front: Photograph: shows a man mid-leap over a puddle behind a Paris train station; the word 'RAILOWSKY' appears twice in the background
Artist: developed the concept of the 'decisive moment' in photography, arguing a photographer must recognize specific instants when visual elements are in balance
Artist: co-founded Magnum Photos with Robert Capa and others
Photograph: looks down from the top of a spiral staircase at a cyclist in motion below; titled after a town in southern France
Artist: photographed Gandhi approximately one hour before his assassination
Work: photobook also known as Images à la sauvette ('Images on the Sly'), with a cover designed by Henri Matisse; documents Gandhi's funeral and the founding of Communist China
Photograph: portrait in which the subject strides behind one of his own Walking Man sculptures, unconsciously imitating its pose
Artist: photographed the last imperial eunuchs in China as part of a series called 'The Last Days of the Kuomintang'; also captured crowds buying gold in Shanghai during the Communist takeover
Artist: used a 35mm Leica camera throughout most of his career
Artist: told William Eggleston that 'color is bullshit'
Artist: had a creative epiphany upon seeing a Martin Munkacsi photograph of three naked children running into the surf of Lake Tanganyika
Artist: assisted filmmaker Jean Renoir on A Day in the Country and The Rules of the Game before his photography career
Artist: said all his job required was 'one finger, one eye, and two legs'
Photograph: shows children playing in ruins; a hole blasted into a wall forms most of the frame; associated with the Spanish Civil War
back: Children Playing in the Ruins (Henri Cartier-Bresson)

front: Photograph: images of a Barong Dance, appearing in Antoine Artaud's book The Dances of Bali
Artist: a 2015 Teju Cole essay titled 'Perfect and Unrehearsed' discusses this photographer's image of three men in a canoe on Suzhou Creek
